findings.summary: Generation handoffs still require repeated seam-level normalization
evidence: Boundary preparation and payload shaping remain distributed across form, transform, and service layers, producing repeated adapters.
Error and async contract differences between adjacent modules still force caller-side normalization at multiple seams.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a single generation-boundary contract module for payload + error semantics, then route these four modules through it to remove duplicated seam glue.
findings.reasoning: Consistent seams improve reliability and reduce maintenance overhead where generation and orchestration pathways intersect.
